message:
fix(coordinator,robot): enforce directory boundary in wildcard glob matching and fix consecutive file path extraction regex overlap

Wildcard glob matchers in both conflicts.go and synthesis.go allowed a
single * to cross directory boundaries — e.g., *.go would incorrectly
match foo/bar.go. This violated POSIX glob semantics where * should
only match within a single path segment.

- Add slash-in-skipped-portion guard to matchesWildcardPattern in
  conflicts.go: when scanning for a literal segment after *, reject
  the match if the skipped text (remaining[:idx]) contains a /
- Add trailing-wildcard guard: if a terminal * produced leftover text
  containing /, reject the match to prevent cross-directory leakage
- Apply identical guards to matchesWildcard in synthesis.go to keep
  both wildcard matcher implementations consistent
- Fix filePathRegex in routing.go: remove trailing boundary group
  (?:[\s"'),:]|$) that consumed whitespace between consecutive paths,
  causing the second path's leading boundary to be eaten by the first
  match — e.g., "src/main.go src/util.go" only extracted the first path
- Add regression test "consecutive paths overlap bug" asserting both
  paths are extracted from "Update src/main.go src/util.go"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
